mn24:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the Squirrels’ Feeding Ground.
mn24:10.1: “When asked, ‘Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification of ethical conduct?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:10.2: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification of mind [*cittavisuddhatthaṁ*]?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:10.3: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification of view?’ …
mn24:10.4: for the sake of purification by overcoming doubt? …
mn24:10.5: for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path? …
mn24:10.6: for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision of the way? …
mn24:10.7: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:10.8: For what purpose then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā*?”
mn24:10.9: “Friend, the holy life is lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānatthaṁ*].”
mn24:11.1: “Then, friend, is purification of ethical conduct complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:11.11: “Then, friend, is purification by knowledge and vision of the way complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:11.13: “Then, friend, is purification by knowledge and vision complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:11.15: “Then, friend, is complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*] something apart from these *dhammas*?”
mn24:11.3: “Then, friend, is purification of mind [*cittavisuddhi*] complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:11.5: “Then, friend, is purification of view complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:11.7: “Then, friend, is purification by overcoming doubt complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:11.9: “Then, friend, is purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?”
mn24:12.1: “When asked, ‘Then, friend, is purification of ethical conduct complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:12.2: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is purification of mind [*cittavisuddhi*] complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:12.3: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is purification of view complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?’ …
mn24:12.5: purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path …
mn24:12.6: purification by knowledge and vision of the way …
mn24:12.7: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is purification by knowledge and vision complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*]?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:12.8: When asked, ‘Then, friend, is complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*] something apart from these *dhammas*?’ you said, ‘No, friend.’
mn24:12.9: How then, friend, should the meaning of this statement be understood?”
mn24:13.1: “Friend, if the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification of ethical conduct to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.2: If the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification of mind [*cittavisuddhi*] to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.3: If the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification of view to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.4: If the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification by overcoming doubt to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.5: If the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.6: If the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification by knowledge and vision of the way to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.7: If the *Bhagavā* were to declare purification by knowledge and vision to be complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*], then while there was still clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], he would be declaring complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*].
mn24:13.8: And if complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānaṁ*] existed apart from these *dhammas*, then an ordinary person would attain final *nibbāna*.
mn24:13.9: For an ordinary person, friend, is apart from these *dhammas*.
mn24:14.1: “So then, friend, I will give you a simile;
mn24:14.10: By means of the sixth relay chariot he would reach the seventh relay chariot; he would dismiss the sixth relay chariot and mount the seventh relay chariot.
mn24:14.11: By means of the seventh relay chariot he would arrive at Sāketa, at the gate of the inner palace.
mn24:14.12: When he had arrived at the gate of the inner palace, his friends, ministers, relatives, and blood kin might ask him:
mn24:14.13: ‘Great king, was it by means of this relay chariot that you arrived from Sāvatthī at Sāketa, at the gate of the inner palace?’
mn24:14.14: How, friend, would King Pasenadi of Kosala answer if he were answering rightly?”
mn24:14.15: “Friend, if King Pasenadi of Kosala were answering rightly, he would answer like this:
mn24:14.16: ‘Here, while I was residing at Sāvatthī, some urgent business arose for me at Sāketa.
mn24:14.17: Then, between Sāvatthī and Sāketa, seven relay chariots were made ready for me.
mn24:14.18: Then I left Sāvatthī and, from the gate of the inner palace, mounted the first relay chariot.
mn24:14.19: By means of the first relay chariot I reached the second relay chariot; I dismissed the first relay chariot and mounted the second relay chariot.
mn24:14.2: for by a simile some wise people here understand the meaning of what is said.
mn24:14.20: By means of the second relay chariot I reached the third relay chariot; I dismissed the second relay chariot and mounted the third relay chariot.
mn24:14.21: By means of the third relay chariot I reached the fourth relay chariot; I dismissed the third relay chariot and mounted the fourth relay chariot.
mn24:14.22: By means of the fourth relay chariot I reached the fifth relay chariot; I dismissed the fourth relay chariot and mounted the fifth relay chariot.
mn24:14.23: By means of the fifth relay chariot I reached the sixth relay chariot; I dismissed the fifth relay chariot and mounted the sixth relay chariot.
mn24:14.24: By means of the sixth relay chariot I reached the seventh relay chariot; I dismissed the sixth relay chariot and mounted the seventh relay chariot.
mn24:14.25: By means of the seventh relay chariot I arrived at Sāketa, at the gate of the inner palace.’
mn24:14.26: That is how King Pasenadi of Kosala would answer if he were answering rightly.”
mn24:14.3: Suppose, friend, that for King Pasenadi of Kosala, while residing at Sāvatthī, some urgent business were to arise at Sāketa.
mn24:14.4: Between Sāvatthī and Sāketa, seven relay chariots would be made ready for him.
mn24:14.5: Then, friend, King Pasenadi of Kosala would leave Sāvatthī and, from the gate of the inner palace, mount the first relay chariot. By means of the first relay chariot he would reach the second relay chariot; he would dismiss the first relay chariot and mount the second relay chariot.
mn24:14.6: By means of the second relay chariot he would reach the third relay chariot; he would dismiss the second relay chariot and mount the third relay chariot.
mn24:14.7: By means of the third relay chariot he would reach the fourth relay chariot; he would dismiss the third relay chariot and mount the fourth relay chariot.
mn24:14.8: By means of the fourth relay chariot he would reach the fifth relay chariot; he would dismiss the fourth relay chariot and mount the fifth relay chariot.
mn24:14.9: By means of the fifth relay chariot he would reach the sixth relay chariot; he would dismiss the fifth relay chariot and mount the sixth relay chariot.
mn24:15.1: “Just so, friend, purification of ethical conduct is only for the sake of purification of mind [*cittavisuddhatthā*]; purification of mind [*cittavisuddhi*] is only for the sake of purification of view; purification of view is only for the sake of purification by overcoming doubt; purification by overcoming doubt is only for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path; purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path is only for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision of the way; purification by knowledge and vision of the way is only for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision; purification by knowledge and vision is only for the sake of complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānatthā*].
mn24:15.2: Friend, the holy life is lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of complete *nibbāna* without clinging [*anupādāparinibbānatthaṁ*].”
mn24:16.1: When this was said, the venerable Sāriputta said this to the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta:
mn24:16.2: “What is your name, venerable sir, and how do your fellow celibates know you?”
mn24:16.4: and my fellow celibates know me as Mantāṇiputta.”
mn24:16.6: Just as might be expected from a learned disciple who truly understands the Teacher’s instruction, so indeed deep questions, profound questions, have been answered one by one by the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta.
mn24:16.7: It is a gain for the fellow celibates, a great gain for the fellow celibates,
mn24:16.8: that they get to see the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, that they get to attend on him.
mn24:16.9: Even if fellow celibates were to carry the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta about on their heads in a cloth sling, so that they might get to see him and attend on him, it would still be a gain for them, a great gain for them. It is also a gain for us, a great gain for us, that we get to see the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, that we get to attend on him.”
mn24:17.1: When this was said, the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta said this to the venerable Sāriputta:
mn24:17.10: It is a gain for the fellow celibates, a great gain for the fellow celibates,
mn24:17.11: that they get to see the venerable Sāriputta, that they get to attend on him.
mn24:17.12: Even if fellow celibates were to carry the venerable Sāriputta about on their heads in a cloth sling, so that they might get to see him and attend on him, it would still be a gain for them, a great gain for them. It is also a gain for us, a great gain for us, that we get to see the venerable Sāriputta, that we get to attend on him.”
mn24:17.13: And so those two great nāgas each rejoiced in the other’s well-spoken words.
mn24:17.14: The Relay Chariots is finished, the fourth.
mn24:17.2: “What is your name, venerable sir, and how do your fellow celibates know you?”
mn24:17.4: and my fellow celibates know me as Sāriputta.”
mn24:17.5: “Indeed, sir, we did not know that we were conversing with a disciple comparable to the Teacher:
mn24:17.7: For if we had known, ‘It is the venerable Sāriputta,’ even this much would not have occurred to us to say.
mn24:17.9: Just as might be expected from a learned disciple who truly understands the Teacher’s instruction, so indeed deep questions, profound questions, have been asked one by one by the venerable Sāriputta.
mn24:2.1: Then several *bhikkhus* from Jātibhūmi, having spent the rains at Jātibhūmi, went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to the *Bhagavā*, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. When they were seated to one side, the *Bhagavā* said this to them:
mn24:2.2: “*Bhikkhus*, who among the *bhikkhus* from Jātibhūmi is regarded by the fellow celibates from Jātibhūmi in this way:
mn24:2.3: ‘One who is himself of few wishes, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on fewness of wishes; who is himself content, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on contentment; who is himself secluded [*pavivitto*], and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on seclusion [*pavivekakathañca*]; who is himself not entangled, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on non-entanglement; who is himself energetic, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on arousing energy; who is himself accomplished in ethical conduct, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on the accomplishment of ethical conduct; who is himself accomplished in *samādhi* [*samādhisampanno*], and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on the accomplishment of *samādhi* [*samādhisampadākathañca*]; who is himself accomplished in discernment [*paññāsampanno*], and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on the accomplishment of discernment [*paññāsampadākathañca*]; who is himself accomplished in liberation, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on the accomplishment of liberation; who is himself accomplished in the knowledge and vision of liberation, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on the accomplishment of the knowledge and vision of liberation; an adviser, an informant, one who makes things clear, one who encourages, one who stirs up, one who gladdens the fellow celibates’”?
mn24:2.4: “Bhante, the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta is regarded by the fellow celibates from Jātibhūmi in this way:
mn24:2.5: ‘One who is himself of few wishes, and who speaks to the *bhikkhus* on fewness of wishes; who is himself content … an adviser, an informant, one who makes things clear, one who encourages, one who stirs up, one who gladdens the fellow celibates.’”
mn24:3.1: Now at that time the venerable Sāriputta was sitting not far from the *Bhagavā*.
mn24:3.2: Then this thought occurred to the venerable Sāriputta:
mn24:3.3: “It is a gain for the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, a great gain for the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta,
mn24:3.4: that wise fellow celibates, right in the Teacher’s presence, speak praise of him again and again after considering him, and the Teacher approves it.
mn24:3.5: Perhaps someday or other I too might meet with the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta; perhaps there might be some conversation.”
mn24:4.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having stayed at Rājagaha as long as he pleased, set out on tour toward Sāvatthī.
mn24:4.2: Touring by stages, he eventually arrived at Sāvatthī.
mn24:4.3: There the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jetavana, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
mn24:4.4: The venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta heard: “It seems the *Bhagavā* has arrived at Sāvatthī; he is dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jetavana, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.”
mn24:5.1: Then the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, having set his lodging in order and taking his bowl and robe, set out on tour toward Sāvatthī.
mn24:5.2: Touring by stages, he eventually arrived at Sāvatthī, at Jetavana, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery, and went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to the *Bhagavā*, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side.
mn24:5.3: When the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta was seated to one side, the *Bhagavā* instructed, encouraged, inspired, and gladdened him with a *dhamma* talk.
mn24:5.4: Then the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, having been instructed, encouraged, inspired, and gladdened by the *Bhagavā* with a *dhamma* talk, welcomed and approved the *Bhagavā*’s words, rose from his seat, paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, and keeping him to his right, went to Andhavana for the day’s abiding.
mn24:6.1: Then a certain *bhikkhu* went to the venerable Sāriputta, and having gone to him, said this:
mn24:6.2: “The *bhikkhu* Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, friend Sāriputta, whom you have often been praising, has been instructed, encouraged, inspired, and gladdened by the *Bhagavā* with a *dhamma* talk; and having welcomed and approved the *Bhagavā*’s words, he rose from his seat, paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, and keeping him to his right, has gone off to Andhavana for the day’s abiding.”
mn24:7.1: Then the venerable Sāriputta, in haste, taking his sitting cloth, followed right behind the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta, keeping him in sight.
mn24:7.2: Then the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta entered Andhavana and sat down at the root of a certain tree for the day’s abiding.
mn24:7.3: The venerable Sāriputta too entered Andhavana and sat down at the root of a certain tree for the day’s abiding.
mn24:8.1: Then in the evening, the venerable Sāriputta rose from retreat and went to the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta. Having gone to him, he exchanged greetings with the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta.
mn24:8.2: When they had concluded their courteous and memorable talk, he sat down to one side. Seated to one side, the venerable Sāriputta said this to the venerable Puṇṇa Mantāṇiputta:
mn24:9.1: “Friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā*?”
mn24:9.11: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision of what is path and not-path?”
mn24:9.13: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision of the way?”
mn24:9.15: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification by knowledge and vision?”
mn24:9.3: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification of ethical conduct?”
mn24:9.5: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification of mind [*cittavisuddhatthaṁ*]?”
mn24:9.7: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification of view?”
mn24:9.9: “Then, friend, is the holy life lived under the *Bhagavā* for the sake of purification by overcoming doubt?”
